pointsAdjustments.reason: Brazilian GP fuel-irregularity ruling: Schumacher's win reinstated for the drivers championship only; constructors points stripped.

setupGuide.notes: AMS2 ships the Buenos Aires No.6 layout used for the 1995 Argentine GP; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: No 1995 Imola layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-adjacent 1988 layout. Imola 1988's AI cap is 25, so the 26-car San Marino entry is capped at 24 opponents plus the player here.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours — 72 laps / 306.0 km reproduced as 73 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue (the f1-1988/1992 pack convention); the 90s-Barcelona suggestion doubles as this season's Spanish GP venue.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1995 Spa layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1993 layout.
AMS2 ships the modern Nürburgring GP-Strecke; the venue is real and the GP layout is largely as raced in 1995, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for the Okayama International Circuit (TI Circuit Aida) — 83 laps / 307.3 km reproduced as 73 laps of Jerez 1988. Aida is not in AMS2; Jerez 1988 is a tight, technical, period-correct permanent circuit of similar rhythm and is free on the 1995 calendar (the Spanish GP runs at Barcelona).
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the classic (pre-2003) layout.
